The system includes a compatibility check that assesses audio/video permissions, internet strength, and other key parameters to ensure a smooth interview experience. You can run a system compatibility check at https://www.hackerrank.com/compatibility. Key benefits of the Zoom integration include: no Zoom account or software installation required, calls connect automatically when an interview starts, high-quality audio/video maintained even on slow connections, and calls automatically reconnect if network drops and is restored within two minutes. If you continue experiencing connectivity issues after running the compatibility check, the problem may be related to your network configuration or firewall settings blocking the Zoom connection. verifier_claims.text: To remove an interviewer from your HackerRank platform, follow these steps:
1. Navigate to your profile icon in the upper-right corner and select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu.
2. Locate the user in the **Users** tab.
3. Click the **three-dot icon** (more options menu) next to the user's name.
4. Select **Remove from Teams** and confirm the action.
Alternatively, you can use the bulk user management feature if you need to remove multiple users at once. Admins can per

response:
To remove an interviewer from your HackerRank platform, follow these steps:

1. Navigate to your profile icon in the upper-right corner and select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu.
2. Locate the user in the **Users** tab.
3. Click the **three-dot icon** (more options menu) next to the user's name.
4. Select **Remove from Teams** and confirm the action.

Alternatively, you can use the bulk user management feature if you need to remove multiple users at once. Admins can perform bulk actions including removing users from teams through the new dropdown menu that consolidates bulk actions.

Source: [Teams Management]
justification: The corpus clearly covers user removal procedures in HackerRank. The first excerpt specifically mentions clicking the three-dot icon and selecting 'Remove from Teams', which directly addresses the user's question about not seeing this option. verifier_claims.text: You can pause your HackerRank subscription instead of canceling it. Here's how:
1. Click on your profile icon in the top-right corner and select Settings
2. Navigate to the Billing section under Subscription
3. Click the Cancel Plan button
4. When the prompt appears about the Pause Subscription feature, select your desired pause duration (minimum 1 month, ma
This feature allows you to temporarily pause your subscription and resume it whenever needed, providing flexibility for

response:
You can pause your HackerRank subscription instead of canceling it. Here's how:

**Prerequisites:**
- You must have an active subscription that started at least 30 days ago
- You must have a monthly subscription for Individual Monthly - Basic or Interview Monthly plans

**Steps to pause:**
1. Click on your profile icon in the top-right corner and select Settings
2. Navigate to the Billing section under Subscription
3. Click the Cancel Plan button
4. When the prompt appears about the Pause Subscription feature, select your desired pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months)

This feature allows you to temporarily pause your subscription and resume it whenever needed, providing flexibility for situations like yours where hiring efforts have stopped temporarily.

Source: Pause Subscription
justification: User is requesting to pause their HackerRank subscription due to stopped hiring efforts. The corpus provides comprehensive coverage of the pause subscription feature including prerequisites, step-by-step instructions, and duration options. This is a standard product feature request that can be fully addressed with the available documentation.

verifier_claims.text: Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. HackerRank allows you to update the name on your certificate only once
1. Open your certificate page
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display
3. Click Regenerate Certificate
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name
The platform will then update your certificate with the new name.

response:
Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. HackerRank allows you to update the name on your certificate only once per account, and when you update your name, the change applies to all your certificates. After you update it, you cannot change it again.

To update the name on your certificate:
1. Open your certificate page
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display
3. Click Regenerate Certificate
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name

The platform will then update your certificate with the new name.

Source: Certifications FAQs
justification: User requests certificate name correction, which is directly covered in the HackerRank Certifications FAQs corpus. The corpus provides clear step-by-step instructions for updating certificate names, including the one-time limitation policy.

verifier_claims.text: Anthropic uses robots to gather data from the public web for model development, web searches, and retrieving web content

response: Anthropic uses robots to gather data from the public web for model development, web searches, and retrieving web content. To stop Claude from crawling your website, you can block the crawler by adding directives to your robots.txt file in your top-level directory. To block ClaudeBot from your entire website, add this to your robots.txt file: User-agent: ClaudeBot Disallow: / You must do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from. Anthropic's bots respect industry standard directives in robots.txt and honor 'do not crawl' signals. To limit crawling activity, you can also use the Crawl-delay extension by adding: User-agent: ClaudeBot Crawl-delay: 1. Source: Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?
justification: Strong domain match with comprehensive coverage of web crawling opt-out procedures. User wants to stop website crawling, and corpus provides specific robots.txt instructions for blocking ClaudeBot.

response:
To remove an employee who has left your company from your HackerRank hiring account, you can use the user management features. As a Company Admin or Team Admin, you have several options:

1. **Remove from Teams**: Click the three-dot icon next to the user's name, select 'Remove from Teams', and confirm the action.

2. **Transfer Ownership**: If the departing employee owned any resources like tests, you can transfer these to another user before removing them.

3. **Bulk User Management**: If you need to remove multiple users at once, admins can perform bulk actions including adding or removing users from teams, or locking accounts.

Note that you must have Company Admin or Team Admin access to perform these user management actions.

Source: User Management
justification: Clear user management question with strong corpus coverage. The excerpts provide specific steps for removing users from teams and mention bulk management options. User appears to be an admin managing their company account.

subject: Claude for students

11725453 set up the claude lti in canvas by instructure :: Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure
index :: Claude for Education
11725453 set up the claude lti in canvas by instructure :: Installing Claude LTI as an App
11725453 set up the claude lti in canvas by instructure :: Creating Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas

verifier_claims.text: To set up Claude LTI for your students in Canvas, you'll need to follow these steps as a Claude for Education administra
**Creating Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas:**
1. Sign in to Canvas as an administrator and go to Admin -> Developer Keys
4. Under Additional Settings, toggle Privacy Level to 'Public'
5. Under Placements, remove defaults and add 'Course Navigation' and 'Assignment Edit'
6. Click 'Save' and toggle the state to 'On'
**Installing Claude LTI as an App:**
1. In Canvas, go to Admin -> Settings -> Apps
2. Click 'View App Configurations' then select '+ App'
3. Select Configuration Type 'By Client ID'
4. Input the Client ID from your developer key (Step 6 above)

response:
To set up Claude LTI for your students in Canvas, you'll need to follow these steps as a Claude for Education administrator and LMS administrator:

**Creating Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas:**
1. Sign in to Canvas as an administrator and go to Admin -> Developer Keys
2. Click '+ Developer Key' then '+ LTI Key'
3. Enter these details:
   - Key Name: Claude LTI
   - Description: Short description for the Canvas LTI 1.3 app
   - Redirect URIs: https://claude.ai/lti/launch
   - Title: Claude LTI
   - Target Link URI: https://claude.ai/lti/launch
   - OpenID Connect Initiation Url: https://claude.ai/api/lti/login
   - JWK method: https://claude.ai/api/lti/keys
4. Under Additional Settings, toggle Privacy Level to 'Public'
5. Under Placements, remove defaults and add 'Course Navigation' and 'Assignment Edit'
6. Click 'Save' and toggle the state to 'On'

**Installing Claude LTI as an App:**
1. In Canvas, go to Admin -> Settings -> Apps
2. Click 'View App Configurations' then select '+ App'
3. Select Configuration Type 'By Client ID'
4. Input the Client ID from your developer key (Step 6 above)
5. Click 'Install' and refresh the course page

This setup requires both Claude for Education administrator access and Canvas LMS administrator permissions.

Source: Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure
justification: Strong domain match with exact topic coverage. User is a professor requesting Claude LTI setup for students, and corpus provides comprehensive step-by-step instructions for Canvas integration. This is a standard product setup question with complete documentation available.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend

evidence.title: support :: Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?
visa rules :: Visa Credit Card Rules & Regulations
visa rules
support :: I have concerns about a place where I used my Visa card. What should I do?
regulations fees :: The Visa system

verifier_claims.text: The merchant is correct. While generally merchants cannot establish minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions, th

response: The merchant is correct. While generally merchants cannot establish minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions, there is a specific exception for the US Virgin Islands (where you are located). In US territories including the US Virgin Islands, Puerto Rico, and Guam, merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10, but only for credit cards. If the merchant is requiring more than $10 minimum or applying this rule to a debit card, you should notify your Visa card issuer. Source: [Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?]
justification: Strong retrieval match directly addresses the user's location-specific question. Corpus explicitly mentions US Virgin Islands as an exception where $10 minimum is allowed for credit cards. This is a straightforward product information request with clear policy coverage.
